data.title: NetEase Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.44 beats by $0.52, revenue of $4B beats by $60M
data.description:
• NetEase press release (NASDAQ:NTES): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.44 beats by $0.52.
  • Revenue of $4B (+7.4% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Games and related value-added services net revenues were RMB24.0 billion (US$3.3 billion), an increase of 12.1% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • Youdao net revenues were RMB1.3 billion (US$178.9 million), a decrease of 6.7% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • NetEase Cloud Music net revenues were RMB1.9 billion (US$256.1 million), a decrease of 8.4% compared with the same quarter of 2024.
  • Innovative businesses and others net revenues were RMB1.6 billion (US$223.8 million), a decrease of 17.6% compared with the same quarter of 2024.

data.title: Youdao Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.09, revenue of $178.9M misses by $4.86M
data.description:
• Youdao press release (NYSE:DAO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.09.
  • Revenue of $178.9M (-6.7% Y/Y) misses by $4.86M.
  • Net revenues from learning services were RMB602.4 million (US$83.0 million), representing a 16.1% decrease from the same period in 2024.
  • Net revenues from smart devices were RMB190.5 million (US$26.3 million), representing a 5.1% increase from the same period in 2024.
  • Net revenues from online marketing services were RMB505.4 million (US$69.6 million), representing a 2.6% increase from the same period in 2024.
  • "Looking ahead, we will accelerate the deployment of our large language model, Confucius, across learning and advertising verticals as part of our AI-native strategy — enhancing user experience while further boosting profitability. Having achieved our first full year of operating profit in 2024, we are well-positioned to sustain this momentum and deliver stronger profit growth and full-year operating cash flow breakeven in 2025," Dr. Zhou concluded.

data.title: Putin won't attend Russia-Ukraine talks in Turkey
data.description:
Russian President Vladimir Putin will not participate in talks with Ukraine in Turkey scheduled for Thursday, rejecting Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy's calls for him to attend.

Putin had called on Ukraine to "resume direct negotiations without any preconditions" on Thursday in Turkey, which U.S. President Donald Trump had backed. Zelenskyy replied that Ukraine was ready to negotiate.

"I will be in Turkey this Thursday, and I expect Putin to come as well. Personally. And I hope that this time, Putin won’t be looking for excuses as to why he 'can't' make it," Zelenskyy said at the time.

But the Kremlin on Wednesday revealed that the Russian delegation traveling to Turkey would not include Putin. It will be led by presidential aide Vladimir Medinsky, and will include Russia's deputy foreign minister, deputy defense minister, and the head of its military intelligence agency.

"Putin is the one who determines everything in Russia, so he is the one who has to resolve the war. This is his war. Therefore, the negotiations should be with him," Zelenskyy insisted. 

Trump—who's currently on a three-day Middle East trip—will not attend the talks in Turkey, after previously saying he might consider it "if it is appropriate." Instead, the talks with be attended by two U.S. envoys and Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

data.title: Eisai GAAP EPS of ¥163.76, revenue of ¥789.4B; initiates FY outlook
data.description:
• Eisai press release (OTCPK:ESALF): FY GAAP EPS of ¥163.76.
  • Revenue of ¥789.4B (+6.4% Y/Y).

data.title: Merck KGaA Non-GAAP EPS of €2.12, revenue of €5.28B; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Merck KGaA press release (OTCPK:MKGAF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €2.12.
  • Revenue of €5.28B (+3.1% Y/Y), up 2.5% organically compared with the year-earlier quarter.
  • EBITDA pre up organically by 5.8% to €1.5 billion.
  • The company now expects Group full-year net sales of between €20.9 billion and €22.4 billion and EBITDA pre of between €5.8 billion and €6.4 billion. This corresponds to organic sales growth of 2% to 6% and an organic EBITDA pre growth of 2% to 7%.

data.title: Mizuho Financial GAAP EPS of ¥350.20, revenue of ¥9030.37B; initiates FY outlook
data.description:
• Mizuho Financial press release (NYSE:MFG): FY GAAP EPS of ¥350.20.
  • Revenue of ¥9030.37B (+3.3% Y/Y).

data.title: ENGIE SA reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ENGIE SA press release (OTCPK:ENGIY): Q1 Revenue of €23.25B (+5.6% Y/Y).
  • EBIT excluding nuclear at €3.7B, an organic increase of 2.1%, mainly driven by Infrastructures and favourable timing effect.
  • Cash Flow From Operations1 at €4.0B in Q1 2025
  • Maintaining a solid balance sheet with an economic net debt/EBITDA ratio down to 3.0x.
  • Economic net debt reduced by €1.8B.
  • FY 2025 guidance confirmed, with NRIgs expected in a range of €4.4-5.0B, with EBIT excluding nuclear in an indicative range of €8.0-9.0B.

data.title: Cisco in charts: Q1 product revenue up 15% Y/Y, driven by 8% growth in Networking and 54% surge in Security
Deutsche Telekom AG Non-GAAP EPS of €0.50, revenue of €29.75B; raises FY outlook
data.description:
• Deutsche Telekom AG press release (OTCQX:DTEGY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.50.
  • Revenue of €29.75B (+6.5% Y/Y).
  • In organic terms, net revenue increased by 3.8 %. Service revenue was up 6.3 % to €25.0 billion. In organic terms, net revenue increased by 3.5 %.
  • Adjusted EBITDA AL grew by 7.9 % to €11.3 billion. In organic terms, it increased by 5.3 %.
  • Cash capex (before spectrum investment) decreased from €4.7 billion to €4.3 billion.
  • Free cash flow AL was up by €1.9 billion to €5.6 billion.
  • Guidance raised for the 2025 financial year: We are raising our guidance for adjusted EBITDA AL and free cash flow AL of the Group for the 2025 financial year. Instead of around €44.9 billion, we now expect to post adjusted EBITDA AL of around €45.0 billion. Instead of the previous level of around €19.9 billion, we now expect to post free cash flow AL of around €20.0 billion.

data.title: Tesla chairwoman Robyn Denholm made $198M in last six months
